recurringPattern: Horizontal top nav bar with company logo left, four center links, and 'Sign In' + 'Get Started' (filled + outline) buttons right. Sticky header with a white/semi-transparent background, subtle bottom border or blur.
designHarm: The logo-home-about-features-pricing-signup pattern makes every AI-generated site feel like a startup template. Users cannot distinguish brands when navigation structure is identical. The dual-button (Sign In/Get Started) implies permission hierarchy that may not be real.
legitimateExceptions: Standard marketing sites where the sign-in/get-started distinction is real (logged-in user vs prospect)
Multi-product sites requiring a universal navigation shell
possibleFixtures: Standard startup nav: logo + 4 links + 2 buttons
Expressive nav that breaks the template: no 'Get Started', off-center branding, unexpected link labels
originalObservations: AI-generated marketing sites universally use the logo-left + links-center + dual-buttons-right pattern.
The link labels are nearly always Home, Features, Pricing, and About or Contact.
The 'Sign In' button is always outline/secondary, 'Get Started' is always filled/primary.
